negative_context.description: Broad exception handler swallows errors without recovery or re-raise. Handler action: 'pass'.
negative_context.rationale: AI agents generate syntactically correct but semantically empty exception handlers (FMEA FM-02, RPN 288). Always catch specific exceptions and either re-raise or handle with recovery logic.

negative_context.canonical_alternative:

# Parameterize differences instead of copy-pasting
negative_context.description: 'function_a' and 'function_b' are 100% similar. Reuse or consolidate instead of duplicating.
negative_context.forbidden_pattern:
# ANTI-PATTERN: Creating a near-duplicate of 'function_a'
# 'function_b' already exists with 100% similarity
negative_context.rationale: AI agents copy-paste and modify instead of parameterizing.

negative_context.canonical_alternative:
# REQUIRED: Only add code that is immediately used
# Remove unused imports and speculative helper functions
negative_context.description: Unreachable or unused code detected. Do not add speculative code.
negative_context.forbidden_pattern:
# ANTI-PATTERN: Unused imports, functions, or constants
import unused_module  # <- never referenced
def helper_just_in_case(): ...  # <- never called
negative_context.rationale: AI agents add 'just in case' code that is never used (FMEA FM-10, RPN 96).
